title: Install, Setup, Smoke, Uninstall, Clear, And Lifecycle Performance Rerun Tasklist
mainGoal: Rerun the end-to-end installer or setup lifecycle under the current single-main-process PM2 fork baseline, verify the scaffold tenant smoke routes locally, stop the runtime cleanly, uninstall the installed project, clear preserved runtime data explicitly, and generate one new lifecycle performance report that captures the full sequence with current timings and pass or fail outcomes.

templateStartTenant.loopbackSmokeMode: Use the local listener on `https://127.0.0.1` with `curl -k` and send `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Ordered by one operator-facing lifecycle pass: lock the current single-fork baseline and clean target first, run the real install or setup path, execute smoke requests with strict local timing, stop and remove the runtime cleanly, explicitly clear preserved data, then publish one matching lifecycle performance report.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: This tasklist is a full lifecycle rerun, not another latency-only exercise. It should reuse the strict local measurement habits from the latest request-latency work, but the final artifact must be one lifecycle performance report covering install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: The latest request-latency report already isolated warm-only route costs under the current `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1` baseline. This rerun should keep that runtime topology, repeat the broader install-to-clear operator flow, and compare the resulting lifecycle timings against the latest successful March 23, 2026 lifecycle report instead of mixing the outcome into the latency-only report.

supersedes.note: That earlier lifecycle tasklist was completed before the dedicated single-fork latency pass. This replacement keeps the same end-to-end shape while updating the baseline, source references, and reporting expectations to match the latest March 23, 2026 runtime state.

sourceReports.topic: Latest successful installer, smoke, uninstall, and purge lifecycle report
Latest single-fork timing reference and strict local measurement protocol
Previous full lifecycle tasklist for install, smoke, uninstall, clear, and report publication
Latest measurement tasklist showing the single-fork, strict-curl timing baseline
Current PM2 runtime shape with `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`
Current smoke-route expectations for the scaffold tenant
Published operator-facing contract for installer, setup, uninstall, and purge-data flows

taskGroups.title: Lock The Rerun Baseline And Disposable Target
taskGroups.goal: Start the rerun from a known-clean, reproducible baseline so the resulting lifecycle performance report is safe to execute and easy to compare against prior March 23, 2026 reports.
pointsOfAttention: The active runtime topology is the user's current single-main-process PM2 fork configuration, not the older clustered shape.
The install-to-uninstall cycle must run in a disposable target or disposable clone, because uninstall removes the installed project tree.
The new lifecycle report should capture current conditions cleanly instead of inheriting residue from the earlier successful follow-up run or the later latency-only run.

tasks.title: Confirm the current single-fork runtime baseline
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the rerun explicitly reflect the current PM2 topology and transport assumptions before any timing data is collected.

subtasks.title: Verify `app/ecosystem.config.js` still uses `exec_mode: "fork"` and `instances: 1`
Record that lifecycle timings from this rerun should be compared directly with later single-fork runs and only carefully with older clustered runs
Keep the strict local request rule from the latest timing pass: use `curl --max-time 1` and treat any slower localhost response as a failed smoke request

tasks.title: Prepare a disposable execution shape and clean pre-run state
tasks.reportedGoal: Avoid destroying the authoring checkout and avoid contaminating the rerun with leftover install, PM2, or data state.

subtasks.title: Choose a disposable machine, VM, container, or cloned checkout for the actual installer or setup run
Keep a second checkout available so `setup/purge-data.sh` can still be run after uninstall removes the installed project tree
Confirm `/opt/ehecatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecatl`, and the root PM2 `Ehecatl` process list are clean before the rerun begins

tasks.title: Define the new lifecycle report boundary
tasks.reportedGoal: Set one clear reporting target so the rerun ends with a single trustworthy performance artifact.

subtasks.title: Plan one new `report/performance` JSON artifact dedicated to this rerun rather than appending notes to an older lifecycle or latency report
Capture environment details, command shapes, absolute timestamps, phase outcomes, and timing measurements for install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear
Include an explicit comparison section or summary note against the latest successful lifecycle report and the latest single-fork latency report

taskGroups.title: Rerun Installer Or Setup With The Scaffold Tenant
taskGroups.goal: Execute the real operator-facing installation path and finish with `www.fakedomain.com` scaffolded under the current permission policy and runtime baseline.
pointsOfAttention: Use `installer.sh` for the true fresh-install validation path unless the rerun is intentionally isolating the direct existing-checkout setup path.
The setup flow still needs to answer the optional first-tenant prompt with `fakedomain.com` so the scaffold host becomes `www.fakedomain.com`.
The resulting lifecycle report should distinguish total installer duration from any visible setup or scaffold subphase duration when those checkpoints are observable.

tasks.title: Run the published install or setup entrypoint
tasks.reportedGoal: Complete one real install path without replacing the documented scripts with ad hoc shortcuts.

subtasks.title: Use `./installer.sh` for the full fresh-install flow, or `./setup/setup.sh` only when the explicit goal is to rerun the existing-checkout setup path
Enter `fakedomain.com` at the first-tenant prompt so setup scaffolds the default `www.fakedomain.com` host
Verify install metadata, runtime directories, CLI publication, and scaffold tenant files exist in the expected locations after setup completes

tasks.title: Capture install and scaffold timings
tasks.reportedGoal: Turn the install or setup phase into measured evidence rather than a simple success claim.

subtasks.title: Measure total installer or setup wall-clock duration
Record notable subphase checkpoints when visible, such as dependency installation, `npm install`, runtime-user creation, and first-tenant scaffold completion
Note whether the run was fully unattended after launch or whether it required manual intervention, retries, or environment repair

taskGroups.title: Run Smoke, Route Timing, And Safe Stop Checks
taskGroups.goal: Prove that the installed runtime can serve the scaffold tenant locally under the current host and TLS assumptions, then stop without lingering supervision or teardown drift.
pointsOfAttention: The smoke transport should follow the latest successful lifecycle and latency reports: local HTTPS on `127.0.0.1` with `curl -k` plus the intended tenant host header.
The baseline smoke set should at minimum cover `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session` for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com`.
Cold-path and warm-path timing should be recorded carefully so the lifecycle report does not repeat the earlier mistake of mixing first-hit spawn cost with steady-state route timings.

tasks.title: Boot the runtime and prove local tenant reachability
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm the installed system answers on the local listener for the scaffold tenant before teardown begins.

subtasks.title: Start the runtime with `cd /opt/ehecatl/app && sudo npm run start` or the equivalent installed-project path under test
Send local requests to `https://127.0.0.1` using `curl -sk --max-time 1` and `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
Record the time to the first successful smoke response after runtime start

tasks.title: Validate scaffold route semantics and collect route timings
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm the smoke pass checks route correctness as well as speed.

subtasks.title: Verify `GET /` redirects to `/htm/index.htm` and `GET /htm/index.htm` plus `GET /htm/cached.htm` return successful HTML responses
Verify `GET /hello` and `GET /session` return successful JSON responses with the expected message and timing-related fields
Capture cold first-hit and warm repeated timings for representative routes without mixing tenant-spawn cost into the warm summary

tasks.title: Stop the runtime and confirm clean shutdown
tasks.reportedGoal: Make shutdown a measured and verified part of the lifecycle rather than an assumed afterthought.

subtasks.title: Stop the runtime with `cd /opt/ehecatl/app && sudo npm run stop` or the equivalent installed-project path under test
Measure shutdown wall-clock duration and record whether PM2 or child-process logs show supervision drift, retries, or hung teardown
Confirm a post-stop local request fails immediately instead of continuing to serve stale traffic

taskGroups.title: Uninstall And Clear Preserved Data Explicitly
taskGroups.goal: Prove that uninstall and clear remain distinct, correct steps: first remove the installed project and metadata, then destroy preserved runtime data only through the deliberate clear path.
pointsOfAttention: Uninstall and clear should stay separate so the report proves the documented preservation boundary still works.
The clear step should be executed from another checkout because uninstall removes the installed project tree needed to run its own scripts.
The final post-run state should show no residual `/opt/ehecatl`, `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecatl`, or root PM2 `Ehecatl` entry.

tasks.title: Run uninstall and verify the preservation boundary
tasks.reportedGoal: Confirm uninstall removes installed artifacts while preserved runtime data remains until the explicit clear step.

subtasks.title: Execute `./setup/uninstall.sh` from the installed project tree
Verify the installed project, CLI symlink, and `install-meta.env` are removed
Verify `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` still exist immediately after uninstall until the clear step runs

tasks.title: Run the explicit clear step and confirm a clean environment
tasks.reportedGoal: Finish the rerun with the same clean post-test state expected from the documented `purge-data` path.

subtasks.title: From another checkout, execute `./setup/purge-data.sh` and provide the required `PURGE` confirmation
Verify `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` are removed after the clear step completes
Confirm no residual tenant data, PM2 process entry, install metadata, or other lifecycle residue remains from the rerun

tasks.title: Capture uninstall and clear timings
tasks.reportedGoal: Measure cleanup cost with the same rigor used for install and smoke timings.

subtasks.title: Measure uninstall wall-clock duration
Measure clear wall-clock duration
Record any manual cleanup, residual file, or PM2 artifact the scripts did not remove automatically

taskGroups.title: Publish The New Lifecycle Performance Report
taskGroups.goal: Turn the rerun into one evidence-backed `report/performance` artifact that captures the current lifecycle behavior and makes the next action obvious.
pointsOfAttention: The output should be one fresh lifecycle performance report, not an edit to a past report and not a substitute latency-only artifact.
The report should state exact timestamps, commands, measured durations, pass or fail results, and any manual intervention or anomaly that changed the run.
If a phase fails, the report should identify the failed phase precisely and either point to an existing follow-up tasklist or call for a new one.

tasks.title: Write the full lifecycle performance artifact
tasks.reportedGoal: Create one new JSON report under `report/performance` that covers the complete rerun sequence.

subtasks.title: Record environment details, command shapes, timeline checkpoints, phase results, and measurements for install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear
Include route samples or summaries that distinguish cold-path from warm-path behavior instead of collapsing them into one ambiguous timing claim
Summarize whether the rerun reproduced the latest successful lifecycle outcome, regressed from it, or improved on it

tasks.title: Convert the report into the next action
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the rerun actionable regardless of whether it passes cleanly or exposes a blocker.

subtasks.title: If every phase passes, mark the current revision as validated for the tested single-fork lifecycle rerun
If any phase fails, record the exact phase, symptom, and next recommended fix or follow-up tasklist entry

executionGateForLifecycleRerun.description: This rerun is complete only when one disposable Linux target can be installed or set up, serve the scaffold tenant `www.fakedomain.com` through loopback with the intended host header and strict local timeout, stop cleanly, uninstall cleanly, clear preserved data explicitly, and produce one new timing-backed lifecycle performance report for the whole sequence.
requiredChecks: The active rerun baseline is confirmed as PM2 `fork` mode with one main instance
A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the `tenant-minimal` host for `www.fakedomain.com`

Loopback smoke proves `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` with `curl --max-time 1` while sending `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
Uninstall removes the installed project, CLI, and install metadata while leaving runtime data present until the explicit clear step
The clear step removes the preserved `/etc/opt/ehecatl`, `/var/opt/ehecatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecatl` trees and leaves no PM2 residue
A new `report/performance` artifact is written for the full install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and clear lifecycle
